# Tauri IPC Commands

All Tauri IPC commands defined in `src-tauri/src/commands/` and their TypeScript callsites under `src/`.

## credentials.rs

| Command | Rust signature | TS callsite(s) | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `probe_alpaca_credentials` | `fn probe_alpaca_credentials() -> Result<ProbeResponse, String>` ([src-tauri/src/commands/credentials.rs:311](src-tauri/src/commands/credentials.rs#L311)) | [src/panels/AlpacaCredentialsModal.tsx:81](src/panels/AlpacaCredentialsModal.tsx#L81)<br>[src/panels/AlpacaCredentialsModal.tsx:449](src/panels/AlpacaCredentialsModal.tsx#L449) | Validate the most-recently-saved Alpaca credentials by issuing a single AAPL 1Day bar request against Alpaca's market-data API.  Reads credentials via `get_provider_credentials("alpaca")` (env → file). Times the round-trip and surfaces the latency to the UI. The response is a tagged union — see `ProbeResponse` above and the TS counterpart in `AlpacaCredentialsModal.tsx`.  Never logs the secret. Returns within ~10s (reqwest timeout). |
| `provider_has_credentials` | `fn provider_has_credentials(provider: &str) -> bool` ([src-tauri/src/commands/credentials.rs:221](src-tauri/src/commands/credentials.rs#L221)) | [src/data/symbolCatalog.ts:168](src/data/symbolCatalog.ts#L168) | Whether stored credentials exist for `provider` (env-var override or the plaintext credential file). Returns only a boolean — never the secret — so the frontend can decide whether to show a "Connect Alpaca" affordance without a live network probe and without touching secret material. Reads the same sources, in the same precedence, as [`get_provider_credentials`]. |
| `set_provider_credentials` | `fn set_provider_credentials(provider: String, key_id: String, secret: String,) -> Result<(), String>` ([src-tauri/src/commands/credentials.rs:142](src-tauri/src/commands/credentials.rs#L142)) | [src/panels/__tests__/AlpacaCredentialsModal.test.tsx:7](src/panels/__tests__/AlpacaCredentialsModal.test.tsx#L7)<br>[src/panels/AlpacaCredentialsModal.tsx:97](src/panels/AlpacaCredentialsModal.tsx#L97)<br>[src/panels/AlpacaCredentialsModal.tsx:497](src/panels/AlpacaCredentialsModal.tsx#L497) | Write a provider's API key ID and secret to the plaintext credential file.  Callable from the TS frontend via Tauri IPC. Idempotent — calling again with new values overwrites the old entry.  # Security - The `secret` parameter is consumed and stored; it is never echoed back. - Do not add any logging that includes `secret`. |

## market.rs

| Command | Rust signature | TS callsite(s) | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `market_fetch_history` | `fn market_fetch_history(state: State<'_, AppState>, provider: String, sym: String, tf: String, count: usize,) -> Result<Vec<BarRow>, String>` ([src-tauri/src/commands/market.rs:200](src-tauri/src/commands/market.rs#L200)) | [src/lib/db.ts:572](src/lib/db.ts#L572) | Fetch up to `count` historical bars for `(provider, sym, tf)`.  Order of operations:   1. Validate `tf`.   2. Look up the adapter; if absent → return `"adapter not registered: …"`      so the TS layer can fall back to the mock.   3. Acquire a rate-limit token for the provider.   4. Call `adapter.fetch_history(...)`.   5. Upsert returned bars into the warm cache.   6. Return the bars to TS.  (Cache-first reads will be added in P4.5 once an adapter exists and we can reason about freshness windows. P4.1 ships a write-through path so the table starts populating as soon as the first real adapter lands.) |
| `market_fetch_history_v2` | `fn market_fetch_history_v2(state: State<'_, AppState>, provider: String, sym: String, quote: String, tf: String, count: usize, // Optional epoch-ms cursor: when present, return the page of bars STRICTLY // OLDER than this timestamp instead of the latest `count`. Declared as a // bare `Option<i64>` command arg — in Tauri 2 a missing field deserializes // to `None` automatically (the `CommandArg` impl for `Option` is the // equivalent of serde's `#[serde(default)]`), so existing callers that omit // `before` keep working. This matches the existing optional-arg convention // in `symbols.rs` (`providers: Option<Vec<String>>`) and `db.rs`. before: Option<i64>,) -> Result<Vec<BarRow>, String>` ([src-tauri/src/commands/market.rs:338](src-tauri/src/commands/market.rs#L338)) | [src/lib/db.ts:564](src/lib/db.ts#L564)<br>[src/lib/db.ts:615](src/lib/db.ts#L615) | Fetch up to `count` historical bars for the canonical `(provider, sym, quote, tf)` tuple, write through to `bars_v2`, and return.  Mirrors the v1 orchestrator but dispatches via the registry's typed handles so each adapter's `fetch_history_pair(sym, quote, ...)` runs — preserving the FROZEN `MarketDataProvider` trait per ADR-0001. v1 stays available for any legacy caller that has not yet threaded `quote` through.  Rate-limit tokens are acquired exactly once (same bucket as v1), so a v1+v2 blend does not double-charge. |
| `market_fetch_history_v2_rejects_unsupported_tf` | `fn market_fetch_history_v2_rejects_unsupported_tf()` ([src-tauri/src/commands/market.rs:618](src-tauri/src/commands/market.rs#L618)) | _(none)_ |  |
| `market_fetch_latest_1m` | `fn market_fetch_latest_1m(state: State<'_, AppState>, provider: String, sym: String, quote: String,) -> Result<Option<BarRow>, String>` ([src-tauri/src/commands/market.rs:493](src-tauri/src/commands/market.rs#L493)) | [src/lib/db.ts:593](src/lib/db.ts#L593) | Fetch the single latest **1-minute** `Bar` for `(provider, sym, quote)`.  ## Why this exists (Fix A — 1h stale-price bug)  The Alpaca chart seed previously fetched `count = 1` of the *chart's* own timeframe. For a 1h chart that returns the last **completed** hourly bar, which can be up to ~59 minutes stale. This command instead fetches the latest completed **1-minute** bar (at most ~60s old). The TS adapter (`seedCurrentBucket` in `src/data/adapters/alpaca.ts`) takes the returned 1m close and re-stamps it onto the *current* chart bucket.  ## Why a dedicated command (not `market_fetch_history_v2`)  `"1m"` is intentionally NOT part of the frozen 4-tier chart `Tf` set (ADR-0002), so the `tf_ms` gate in `market_fetch_history_v2_impl` rejects it (`unsupported timeframe`). This command forces the `1m`/`1Min` timeframe directly and **bypasses that gate**, while otherwise mirroring the v2 dispatch + rate-limit + provider-routing. It is the only freshness path that hits Alpaca's `1Min` REST timeframe.  Alpaca is the requirement (equity seed). The crypto adapters' inherent `fetch_history_pair` already accepts an arbitrary tf string, so they are supported trivially here too — but the chart layer only calls this on the Alpaca/equity path.  The latest 1m bar is NOT written to either warm cache: `bars`/`bars_v2` are keyed by the 4-tier `Tf` set, and a `1m` row would pollute those tables. |
| `reload_provider` | `fn reload_provider(state: State<'_, AppState>, provider: String,) -> Result<(), String>` ([src-tauri/src/commands/market.rs:268](src-tauri/src/commands/market.rs#L268)) | [src/panels/AlpacaCredentialsModal.tsx:98](src/panels/AlpacaCredentialsModal.tsx#L98)<br>[src/panels/AlpacaCredentialsModal.tsx:457](src/panels/AlpacaCredentialsModal.tsx#L457)<br>[src/panels/AlpacaCredentialsModal.tsx:529](src/panels/AlpacaCredentialsModal.tsx#L529) | Re-read credentials for `provider` and re-register the adapter.  This allows the frontend to save credentials via `set_provider_credentials` and immediately pick them up without an app restart. Currently only `"alpaca"` is supported; other providers return `Ok(())` as a no-op.  Hot-reload approach chosen over restart-required:   - Alpaca credentials are small and cheap to load from the credentials file.   - Re-registering an adapter is a simple lock-acquire + field update.   - The alternative (requiring restart) would leave the credentials banner     visible and the user without any confirmation that the save worked. |

| `terminal_kill` | `fn terminal_kill(state: State<'_, TerminalState>, session_id: String,) -> Result<(), String>` ([src-tauri/src/commands/terminal.rs:386](src-tauri/src/commands/terminal.rs#L386)) | [src/terminal/terminalClient.ts:204](src/terminal/terminalClient.ts#L204)<br>[src/terminal/terminalClient.ts:226](src/terminal/terminalClient.ts#L226) | Terminate a PTY session by session ID.  Idempotent — returns `Ok(())` even if the session is already gone. Sends a kill signal to the child; the reader task will drain to EOF and emit `terminal:exit` naturally, then remove the session from state. |
| `terminal_resize` | `fn terminal_resize(state: State<'_, TerminalState>, args: TerminalResizeArgs,) -> Result<(), String>` ([src-tauri/src/commands/terminal.rs:350](src-tauri/src/commands/terminal.rs#L350)) | [src/terminal/terminalClient.ts:195](src/terminal/terminalClient.ts#L195) | Resize the terminal window for an active PTY session.  Returns `Err("unknown_session")` if the session_id is not found. |
| `terminal_spawn` | `fn terminal_spawn(app: AppHandle, state: State<'_, TerminalState>, args: TerminalSpawnArgs,) -> Result<TerminalSpawnResult, String>` ([src-tauri/src/commands/terminal.rs:131](src-tauri/src/commands/terminal.rs#L131)) | [src/terminal/terminalClient.ts:156](src/terminal/terminalClient.ts#L156) | Spawn a new interactive `claude` CLI PTY session.  Returns a `session_id` UUID that subsequent `terminal_write`, `terminal_resize`, and `terminal_kill` commands use to address this session.  Errors if the session cap of 4 is reached. |
| `terminal_write` | `fn terminal_write(state: State<'_, TerminalState>, args: TerminalWriteArgs,) -> Result<(), String>` ([src-tauri/src/commands/terminal.rs:311](src-tauri/src/commands/terminal.rs#L311)) | [src/terminal/terminalClient.ts:188](src/terminal/terminalClient.ts#L188) | Write raw bytes (base64-encoded) to a PTY session's master input.  Returns `Err("unknown_session")` if the session_id is not found. |

## src-tauri/src/profile.rs

| Command | Rust signature | TS callsite(s) | Purpose |

| `claude_test_connection` | `fn claude_test_connection(cli_path_override: Option<String>,) -> Result<String, String>` ([src-tauri/src/profile.rs:494](src-tauri/src/profile.rs#L494)) | [src/lib/db.ts:630](src/lib/db.ts#L630) | Tauri command — resolve the `claude` binary path, spawn `<cli> --version`, and return the trimmed version string.  Used by `FirstRun.tsx` and `SettingsPanel.tsx` to confirm the CLI is reachable before showing the main workspace.  * Resolves via `resolve_cli_path_inner` (override → which → ~/.local/bin → ~/.claude/local). * Sets `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` to the isolated profile dir so the smoke test   exercises the same env shape as a real spawn. * 5-second timeout; non-zero exit → `"CliRuntime: <first stderr line>"`. * Not found → `"CliNotFound"`. |
| `profile_auth_status` | `fn profile_auth_status(cli_path: Option<String>) -> Result<AuthStatus, String>` ([src-tauri/src/profile.rs:347](src-tauri/src/profile.rs#L347)) | [src/lib/db.ts:883](src/lib/db.ts#L883) | Tauri command — query `claude auth status --json` under the isolated profile and translate it into the [`AuthStatus`] surface. On any CLI failure (non-zero exit, JSON parse error) falls back to inspecting `<claude-home>/settings.json` for an API key — an outdated CLI without `auth status` should still let the UI render. |
| `profile_init` | `fn profile_init() -> Result<String, String>` ([src-tauri/src/profile.rs:75](src-tauri/src/profile.rs#L75)) | [src/lib/db.ts:845](src/lib/db.ts#L845) |  |
| `profile_login` | `fn profile_login(window: tauri::Window, state: tauri::State<'_, LoginState>, cli_path: Option<String>,) -> Result<(), String>` ([src-tauri/src/profile.rs:153](src-tauri/src/profile.rs#L153)) | [src/lib/db.ts:868](src/lib/db.ts#L868) | Tauri command — start `claude auth login --claudeai` under the isolated profile, streaming stdout/stderr lines to the UI via the `auth:login:line` event. Single-flight: returns `Err("login already in progress")` if a prior invocation hasn't completed. |
| `profile_login_cancel` | `fn profile_login_cancel(state: tauri::State<'_, LoginState>) -> Result<(), String>` ([src-tauri/src/profile.rs:265](src-tauri/src/profile.rs#L265)) | [src/lib/db.ts:872](src/lib/db.ts#L872) | Tauri command — kill the in-flight `claude auth login` child, if any. Idempotent: a no-op when the slot is empty. |

Mirrors the read-modify-write merge of [`profile_set_api_key`] so sibling keys (`hooks`, `disabledSkills`, other env vars) are preserved. We do NOT touch `.claude.json` — the CLI owns that file. |
| `profile_paths` | `fn profile_paths() -> Result<ProfilePaths, String>` ([src-tauri/src/profile.rs:463](src-tauri/src/profile.rs#L463)) | [src/lib/db.ts:850](src/lib/db.ts#L850) |  |
| `profile_set_api_key` | `fn profile_set_api_key(key: String) -> Result<(), String>` ([src-tauri/src/profile.rs:437](src-tauri/src/profile.rs#L437)) | [src/lib/db.ts:855](src/lib/db.ts#L855) | Wave 0 — write a user-supplied API key into `<claude-home>/settings.json`'s `env` block so the CLI sees it on next spawn (without leaking via process env vars when `env_remove` is applied). Read-modify-write merge so existing keys (`hooks`, `disabledSkills`) are preserved. |
